name: wifite
title: wifite

commands.name: wifite
commands.description: Python script to automate wireless auditing using aircrack-ng tools

arguments.description: show this help message and exit
arguments.raw: -h, --help                    show this help message and exit

arguments.description: Shows more options (-h -v). Prints commands and outputs. (default: quiet)
arguments.raw: -v, --verbose                 Shows more options (-h -v). Prints commands and outputs. (default: quiet)

arguments.description: Wireless interface to use, e.g. wlan0mon (default: ask)
arguments.raw: -i [interface]                Wireless interface to use, e.g. wlan0mon (default: ask)

arguments.description: Wireless channel to scan e.g. 1,3-6 (default: all 2Ghz channels)
arguments.raw: -c [channel]                  Wireless channel to scan e.g. 1,3-6 (default: all 2Ghz channels)

arguments.description: Enable infinite attack mode. Modify scanning time with -p (default: off)
arguments.raw: -inf, --infinite              Enable infinite attack mode. Modify scanning time with -p (default: off)

arguments.description: Randomize wireless card MAC address (default: off)
arguments.raw: -mac, --random-mac            Randomize wireless card MAC address (default: off)

arguments.description: Pillage: Attack all targets after scan_time (seconds)
arguments.raw: -p [scan_time]                Pillage: Attack all targets after scan_time (seconds)

arguments.description: Kill processes that conflict with Airmon/Airodump (default: off)
arguments.raw: --kill                        Kill processes that conflict with Airmon/Airodump (default: off)

arguments.description: Attacks any targets with at least min_power signal strength
arguments.raw: -pow, --power [min_power]     Attacks any targets with at least min_power signal strength

arguments.description: Skip cracking captured handshakes/pmkid (default: off)
arguments.raw: --skip-crack                  Skip cracking captured handshakes/pmkid (default: off)

arguments.description: Attacks the first attack_max targets
arguments.raw: -first, --first [attack_max]  Attacks the first attack_max targets

arguments.description: Hides previously-cracked targets. (default: off)
arguments.raw: -ic, --ignore-cracked         Hides previously-cracked targets. (default: off)

arguments.description: Only show targets that have associated clients (default: off)
arguments.raw: --clients-only                Only show targets that have associated clients (default: off)

arguments.description: Passive mode: Never deauthenticates clients (default: deauth targets)
arguments.raw: --nodeauths                   Passive mode: Never deauthenticates clients (default: deauth targets)

arguments.description: Puts device back in managed mode after quitting (default: off)
arguments.raw: --daemon                      Puts device back in managed mode after quitting (default: off)

arguments.description: Show only WEP-encrypted networks
arguments.raw: --wep                         Show only WEP-encrypted networks

arguments.description: Fails attacks if fake-auth fails (default: off)
arguments.raw: --require-fakeauth            Fails attacks if fake-auth fails (default: off)

arguments.description: Retain .IVS files and reuse when cracking (default: off)
arguments.raw: --keep-ivs                    Retain .IVS files and reuse when cracking (default: off)

arguments.description: Show only WPA/WPA2-encrypted networks (may include WPS)
arguments.raw: --wpa                         Show only WPA/WPA2-encrypted networks (may include WPS)

arguments.description: Show only WPA3-encrypted networks (SAE/OWE)
arguments.raw: --wpa3                        Show only WPA3-encrypted networks (SAE/OWE)

arguments.description: Show only OWE-encrypted networks (Enhanced Open)
arguments.raw: --owe                         Show only OWE-encrypted networks (Enhanced Open)

arguments.description: Captures new handshakes, ignores existing handshakes in hs (default: off)
arguments.raw: --new-hs                      Captures new handshakes, ignores existing handshakes in hs (default: off)

arguments.description: File containing passwords for cracking (default: /usr/share/dict/wordlist-probable.txt)
arguments.raw: --dict [file]                 File containing passwords for cracking (default: /usr/share/dict/wordlist-probable.txt)

arguments.description: Show only WPS-enabled networks
arguments.raw: --wps                         Show only WPS-enabled networks

arguments.description: Only use WPS PIN & Pixie-Dust attacks (default: off)
arguments.raw: --wps-only                    Only use WPS PIN & Pixie-Dust attacks (default: off)

arguments.description: Use bully program for WPS PIN & Pixie-Dust attacks (default: reaver)
arguments.raw: --bully                       Use bully program for WPS PIN & Pixie-Dust attacks (default: reaver)

arguments.description: Use reaver program for WPS PIN & Pixie-Dust attacks (default: reaver)
arguments.raw: --reaver                      Use reaver program for WPS PIN & Pixie-Dust attacks (default: reaver)

arguments.description: Do not stop WPS PIN attack if AP becomes locked (default: stop)
arguments.raw: --ignore-locks                Do not stop WPS PIN attack if AP becomes locked (default: stop)

arguments.description: Only use PMKID capture, avoids other WPS & WPA attacks (default: off)
arguments.raw: --pmkid                       Only use PMKID capture, avoids other WPS & WPA attacks (default: off)

arguments.description: Don't use PMKID capture (default: off)
arguments.raw: --no-pmkid                    Don't use PMKID capture (default: off)

arguments.description: Time to wait for PMKID capture (default: 300 seconds)
arguments.raw: --pmkid-timeout [sec]         Time to wait for PMKID capture (default: 300 seconds)

arguments.description: Print previously-cracked access points
arguments.raw: --cracked                     Print previously-cracked access points

arguments.description: Print ignored access points
arguments.raw: --ignored                     Print ignored access points

arguments.description: Check a .cap file (or all hs/*.cap files) for WPA handshakes
arguments.raw: --check [file]                Check a .cap file (or all hs/*.cap files) for WPA handshakes

arguments.description: Show commands to crack a captured handshake
arguments.raw: --crack                       Show commands to crack a captured handshake

arguments.description: Update the local MAC address prefix database from IEEE registries
arguments.raw: --update-db                   Update the local MAC address prefix database from IEEE registries

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# wifite -h
  .               .    
.´  ·  .     .  ·  `.  wifite2 2.8.1
:  :  :  (¯)  :  :  :  a wireless auditor by derv82
`.  ·  ` /¯\ ´  ·  .´  maintained by kimocoder
  `     /¯¯¯\     ´    https://github.com/kimocoder/wifite2
 
 options:
 -h, --help                    show this help message and exit
 
 SETTINGS:
 -v, --verbose                 Shows more options (-h -v). Prints commands and outputs. (default: quiet)
 -i [interface]                Wireless interface to use, e.g. wlan0mon (default: ask)
 -c [channel]                  Wireless channel to scan e.g. 1,3-6 (default: all 2Ghz channels)
 -inf, --infinite              Enable infinite attack mode. Modify scanning time with -p (default: off)
 -mac, --random-mac            Randomize wireless card MAC address (default: off)
 -p [scan_time]                Pillage: Attack all targets after scan_time (seconds)
 --kill                        Kill processes that conflict with Airmon/Airodump (default: off)
 -pow, --power [min_power]     Attacks any targets with at least min_power signal strength
 --skip-crack                  Skip cracking captured handshakes/pmkid (default: off)
 -first, --first [attack_max]  Attacks the first attack_max targets
 -ic, --ignore-cracked         Hides previously-cracked targets. (default: off)
 --clients-only                Only show targets that have associated clients (default: off)
 --nodeauths                   Passive mode: Never deauthenticates clients (default: deauth targets)
 --daemon                      Puts device back in managed mode after quitting (default: off)
 
 WEP:
 --wep                         Show only WEP-encrypted networks
 --require-fakeauth            Fails attacks if fake-auth fails (default: off)
 --keep-ivs                    Retain .IVS files and reuse when cracking (default: off)
 
 WPA:
 --wpa                         Show only WPA/WPA2-encrypted networks (may include WPS)
 --wpa3                        Show only WPA3-encrypted networks (SAE/OWE)
 --owe                         Show only OWE-encrypted networks (Enhanced Open)
 --new-hs                      Captures new handshakes, ignores existing handshakes in hs (default: off)
 --dict [file]                 File containing passwords for cracking (default: /usr/share/dict/wordlist-probable.txt)
 
 WPS:
 --wps                         Show only WPS-enabled networks
 --wps-only                    Only use WPS PIN & Pixie-Dust attacks (default: off)
 --bully                       Use bully program for WPS PIN & Pixie-Dust attacks (default: reaver)
 --reaver                      Use reaver program for WPS PIN & Pixie-Dust attacks (default: reaver)
 --ignore-locks                Do not stop WPS PIN attack if AP becomes locked (default: stop)
 
 PMKID:
 --pmkid                       Only use PMKID capture, avoids other WPS & WPA attacks (default: off)
 --no-pmkid                    Don't use PMKID capture (default: off)
 --pmkid-timeout [sec]         Time to wait for PMKID capture (default: 300 seconds)
 
 COMMANDS:
 --cracked                     Print previously-cracked access points
 --ignored                     Print ignored access points
 --check [file]                Check a .cap file (or all hs/*.cap files) for WPA handshakes
 --crack                       Show commands to crack a captured handshake
 --update-db                   Update the local MAC address prefix database from IEEE registries

examples.command:
wifite -pow 50 -wps

apt_metadata.depends: python3:any, aircrack-ng, reaver, tshark, net-tools, ieee-data, python3-chardet
apt_metadata.description:
Python script to automate wireless auditing using aircrack-ng tools
Wifite is a tool to audit WEP or WPA encrypted wireless networks.
It uses aircrack-ng, pyrit, reaver, tshark tools to perform the audit.
.
This tool is customizable to be automated with only a few arguments
and can be trusted to run without supervision.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
